description: HP 8657B synthesized signal generator (100 kHz to 2060 MHz). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument using two-letter function/units program codes, NOT SCPI. It is listen-only: the Signal Generator cannot talk, so it has no query forms and no identify query. Carrier parameters use a Function-Data-Units sequence (e.g. FR 123.4 MZ); modulation parameters use a Source-Function-Data-Units sequence. Program codes may be upper or lower case. Pulse modulation (PM/PF) and the RF DEAD (R5) feature are available on the 8657B only.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Carrier frequency function entry. Follow with a numeric value and a frequency unit code (HZ, KZ, or MZ). Up to 10 digits. Default after Clear: 100.00000 MHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 100 kHz to 2060 MHz (10 kHz with uncalibrated output)

commands.name: amplitude
commands.description: Carrier output amplitude function entry. Follow with a numeric value (minus sign allowed) and an amplitude unit code. Default after Clear: -143.5 dBm.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: +17 dBm to -143.5 dBm into 50 ohm (DM=dBm, DB=dB, DF=dBf, EM=EMF, VL=Volts, MV=mV, UV=uV)

commands.name: amplitude_offset
commands.description: Amplitude offset function entry.

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Amplitude modulation depth function entry. Used with a modulation source qualifier (S1/S2/S3/S4). Up to 3 digits. Units in percent (PC or %). Default after Clear: 0%.

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Frequency modulation peak deviation function entry. Used with a modulation source qualifier (S1/S2/S3/S4). Up to 3 digits. Default after Clear: 0.0 kHz.

parameters.name: deviation
parameters.range: 0 to 200 kHz (band dependent: 3-100 kHz at 0.1-1040 MHz, 6-200 kHz at 1040-2060 MHz)

commands.name: dc_fm
commands.description: DC FM function entry / modulation source qualifier.

commands.name: pulse_modulation
commands.description: Pulse modulation function entry (8657B only; available as an option). Two pulse modulation modes can be selected (see also PF for fast mode).

commands.name: pulse_modulation_fast
commands.description: Pulse modulation, fast mode (8657B only).

commands.name: modulation_source_external
commands.description: Selects the external modulation source (source qualifier).

commands.name: modulation_source_int_400hz
commands.description: Selects the internal 400 Hz modulation source (source qualifier).

commands.name: modulation_source_int_1khz
commands.description: Selects the internal 1 kHz modulation source (source qualifier).

commands.name: modulation_source_off
commands.description: Turns the modulation source ON / OFF qualifier (Table 3-5: Modulation Source Off; Table 3-4: Modulation Source ON).

commands.name: rf_off
commands.description: RF OFF (function feature).

commands.name: rf_on
commands.description: RF ON (function feature).

commands.name: rf_dead
commands.description: RF DEAD: switches in all attenuator pads for reduced spurs (8657B only).

commands.name: standby
commands.description: Places the Signal Generator in the STBY (standby) state.

commands.name: on
commands.description: Places the Signal Generator in the ON state.

commands.name: reverse_power_protection_reset
commands.description: Resets the reverse power protection circuitry after the source of reverse power has been removed.

commands.name: hi_alc
commands.description: HI ALC function feature (not available on the HP 8656B).

commands.name: lo_alc
commands.description: LO ALC function feature (not available on the HP 8656B).

commands.name: increment_set
commands.description: Increment Set (function qualifier). Sets the step size for the currently selected function; data length is the same as the selected function. Used with the selected function's units.

parameters.name: increment
parameters.range: same as for the selected function

commands.name: step_up
commands.description: Step Up: increments the active function by the increment-set value.

commands.name: step_down
commands.description: Step Down: decrements the active function by the increment-set value.

commands.name: phase_increment
commands.description: Phase Increment (function feature).

commands.name: phase_decrement
commands.description: Phase Decrement (function feature).

commands.name: save
commands.description: Save the current setup into a register (0-9).

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Recall a saved setup from a register (0-9).

commands.name: save_extended
commands.description: Save the current setup into an extended register (0-99).

commands.name: recall_extended
commands.description: Recall a saved setup from an extended register (0-99).

commands.name: sequence
commands.description: Sequence feature: steps through saved setups. Default sequence counter after Clear: 0.

commands.name: flexible_sequence
commands.description: Flexible Sequence (Go To) feature.

commands.name: reverse_sequence
commands.description: Reverse Sequence feature: steps backward through the sequence.
